output:
Thank you for considering us to plan your 5-day Tokyo adventure! To tailor the perfect itinerary and guarantees we stay within your $2,500 budget, I’d like to start with a few clarifying questions:

1. Departure city and preferred travel dates (are you flexible by a day or two)?  
2. Solo traveler or party (number of travelers, ages)?  
3. Hotel style preference (business-class, boutique, or budget)?  
4. Any must-see attractions or special interests (museums, pop-culture, food tours)?  
5. Dietary restrictions or mobility concerns?  

Once I have those details, I can finalize pricing. Below is a provisional plan based on a U.S. departure (New York JFK), early May travel, one adult traveler, mid-range hotel, mix of cultural and shopping activities.

1. Estimated Cost Summary (per person)  
   • Round-trip economy airfare (JFK–NRT on ANA/JAL/United): $900  
   • Hotel (5 nights, mid-range 3★/4★): $120–$160/night → $600–$800  
   • Local transport (Suica card + 72-hr Metro & one Narita Express ticket): $70  
   • Meals (breakfast/lunch/dinner at casual cafés & street stalls): $40/day → $200  
   • Activities & entry fees (museums, teamLab, day tours): $250  
   • Miscellaneous (wifi rental, tips for guides, souvenirs): $80  
   Estimated total: $2,100–$2,300  

2. Hotel Options (nightly rates, approximate)  
   • Tokyu Stay Shinjuku: $120/night – Great location, kitchenette, onsen bath on site  
   • Hotel Gracery Asakusa: $140/night – Modern rooms, 5-min walk to Senso-ji Temple  
   • Shibuya Excel Hotel Tokyu: $160/night – Above Shibuya Station with skyline views  

3. Transportation Plan  
   • Airline choice: ANA or JAL for direct service; consider United via SFO for cost savings.  
   • Airport transfer: Narita Express (round-trip JR pass not needed) ~¥4,000 each way.  
   • In-city: Load a Suica card (~¥2,000 deposit/credit) for subway, JR Yamanote Line, buses.  
   • Optional: Tokyo Metro 72-hour pass (~¥1,500) if you plan heavy sightseeing.  
   • Tips: avoid rush-hour trains (7–9 am, 5–7 pm) and queue neatly at platform markers.  

4. Tokyo “Brochure” Essentials  
   • Local Customs: bow to greet, remove shoes in genkan/home, no tipping.  
   • Key Points of Interest: Senso-ji (Asakusa), Meiji Shrine, Tsukiji Outer Market, teamLab Borderless, Shibuya Crossing, Odaiba waterfront.  
   • Cultural Etiquette: speak quietly on public transit, handle money in trays, line up patiently.  
   • Regulations: carry passport at all times; drinking age 20; smoking only in designated zones.  
   • Passport/Visa: U.S. citizens visa-exempt up to 90 days; passport valid ≥6 months recommended.  

5. 5-Day Sample Itinerary  
   Day 1 (Arrival)  
    Morning: Depart JFK → Arrive NRT early evening  
    Afternoon: Narita Express → hotel check-in, unpack  
    Evening: Dinner in Kabukichō (Shinjuku), stroll Omoide Yokocho  

   Day 2 (Tradition & History)  
    Morning: Senso-ji Temple & Nakamise Shopping Street (Asakusa)  
    Afternoon: Sumida River cruise → Tokyo Skytree observation deck  
    Evening: Izakaya dinner in Kappabashi (kitchenware district)  

   Day 3 (Culture & Pop)  
    Morning: Meiji Shrine & Yoyogi Park  
    Afternoon: Harajuku Takeshita Street → Omotesandō boutique shopping  
    Evening: Shibuya Crossing photos → Hachikō statue → ramen at Ichiran  

   Day 4 (Modern Tokyo)  
    Morning: Tsukiji Outer Market food tour  
    Afternoon: Ginza department stores → Kabuki-za gallery viewing  
    Evening: teamLab Planets or Borderless digital art museum (Odaiba)  

   Day 5 (Flex & Departure)  
    Morning: Free time (Akihabara electronics, Ueno Park museums)  
    Afternoon: Last-minute shopping (Don Quijote, Muji) → hotel check-out  
    Evening: Narita Express → flight home  

6. Promotional Package Option  
   Tokyo “All-In” Experience Package – $350 per person  
    • 72-hr Tokyo Metro & JR East pass  
    • 3 guided half-day tours: Asakusa cultural walk, Tsukiji tasting tour, Shibuya/Harajuku highlights  
    • teamLab Borderless skip-the-line ticket  
    • Pocket Wi-Fi rental for 5 days  
   (Group discounts available for parties of 2+.)  

7. Detailed Cost Breakdown (per person)  
   • Airfare: $900  
   • Hotel (5×$140 average): $700  
   • Transport (Suica + N’EX + Metro pass): $70  
   • Meals: $200  
   • Activities (entry fees & guided tours): $250  
   • Promotional “All-In” Package (optional): $350  
   • Miscellaneous buffer: $30  
   Estimated grand total without package: $2,120  
   With package: $2,470  

Budget Fit: Even with the “All-In” package, your trip comes to approximately $2,470—comfortably within your $2,500 limit. We still have a small cushion for upgrades or special experiences.  

Next Steps: Once you confirm your departure city, exact dates, traveler count and preferences, I’ll lock in airfares, hotel availability and tour reservations. Please let me know, and we’ll have your Tokyo journey secured today!
